metadata.title: Prompt 4.7

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Old Claude inferred your intent; 4.7 does exactly what you type, so vague prompts now produce vague results. Replace vague verbs with actual scope: flag risks per clause, rate severity, suggest rewrites, return a table. Define length explicitly, use positive instructions only, and use action verbs that each ship something specific. 7 calls fewer tools and sounds more direct, so ask for aggressive search, warmth, or 'go beyond the basics' explicitly.

artifactLines: Old Claude inferred your intent; 4.7 does exactly what you type, so vague prompts now produce vague results.
Replace vague verbs with actual scope: flag risks per clause, rate severity, suggest rewrites, return a table.
Define length explicitly, use positive instructions only, and use action verbs that each ship something specific.
7 calls fewer tools and sounds more direct, so ask for aggressive search, warmth, or 'go beyond the basics' explicitly.

beats.narration_text: I want to actually feel the shift from a model that reads between the lines to one that does exactly what I typed. Take my lazy request 'review this contract' and rebuild it into a real 4.7 prompt using all four rules at once. Show me (1) the vague verb rewritten into named scope with per-clause risk flags, severity one-to-five, and a table output, (2) an explicit length constraint added, and (3) one 'not X' instruction flipped into a positive action-verb version.

props.runningText: reading the 31-page PDF so you don't have to…

beats.narration_text: The biggest shift: old Claude read between the lines. If you said 'review this contract' it would figure out what kind of review you wanted. New Claude does exactly what you typed. Which means vague prompts now produce vague results with surgical precision. The fix is the same for every rule that follows: name everything. Outputs, order, boundaries, format, length.

props.artifactHeading: The one rule that changes everything
artifactLines: 4.6: read between the lines, filled in what you meant.
4.7: does exactly what you typed. No more, no less.
Vague prompt → vague result, every time, with precision.
Fix: name every output, name the order, name the format.

beats.narration_text: Four rules that fix most prompts. One: replace vague verbs with actual scope — not 'review this contract' but 'flag risks per clause, rate severity one to five, suggest one rewrite per risky clause, return as a table.' Two: define length explicitly — five bullets, each under fifteen words. Three: use positive instructions only — don't say what not to do, say exactly what to do instead. Four: use action verbs only — each verb tells 4.7 to ship something specific.

props.title: prompt-47-rules.md
props.code: # 4 rules for Claude 4.7 1. Scope: name every output + order + boundaries. Old: Review this contract. New: Flag risks per clause. Rate severity 1-5. Suggest one rewrite. Return as a table. 2. Length: be explicit. Old: Summarize this report. New: Exactly 5 bullets. Each under 15 words. 3. Positive only: say what TO do. Old: Don't use jargon. New: Plain English a 16-year-old reads aloud. 4. Action verbs: each one ships something.

beats.narration_text: Three more. Five: tools — 4.7 calls fewer tools by default, it reasons more between calls. If you need aggressive web search, ask for it explicitly: 'use web search aggressively, verify every claim with at least two sources.' Six: tone — 4.7 is more direct, fewer emojis, almost zero 'great question' energy. If you want warmth back, ask for it or paste example sentences. Seven: on creative tasks, add 'go beyond the basics' — it unlocks the model.

artifactLines: 5. Tools: if you need web search, say so explicitly.
6. Tone: 4.7 is direct by default. Add warmth instructions if needed.
7. Creative tasks: add 'go beyond the basics' to unlock the model.

beats.narration_text: The cleanest before-and-after: the Gmail action email. Before, you'd type 'can you help me with the email?' 4.7 gives you something generic. After, using action verbs and scope: 'Go to my Gmail. Find this contact and read our last conversation. Write the answer email — final draft, send-ready. Goal: book a meeting with the CRO of Snowflake by Friday. Length: under ninety words. Tone: confident, casual, specific.' That's a prompt that ships something.

props.title: email-action-prompt.md
props.code:
Go to my Gmail.
Find [contact] and read our last conversation.
Write the answer email. Final draft. Send-ready.
Goal: book a meeting with the CRO of Snowflake
by Friday.
Length: under 90 words.
Tone: confident, casual, specific.

beats.narration_text: Quick reminder on where to access 4.7. In the Claude app, bottom right corner of the message box, you'll see the model picker. Select Opus 4.7. Then turn on Adaptive thinking. That's the model behind the thirty-one-page prompting guide. You don't need to read the guide. You just read this.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Take one prompt you use every week. Rewrite it using the seven rules. Scope, length, positive, action verbs, tool instructions, tone, and go beyond the basics for anything creative. Paste it here.

props.command: Here is a prompt I use regularly: [paste your prompt]. Rewrite it for Claude Opus 4.7 using these rules: name every output, define length, use positive instructions only, use action verbs, and add explicit tone instructions. Return old vs. new side by side.
